title: citrus medica subf. dulcis
intro: Citrus medica subf. dulcis, also known as the citron, is a species of citrus fruit that is characterized by its thick, leathery rind and fragrant pulp. The citron has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and limonoids. The health benefits of citron include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Citron can be prepared in various ways, including being dried to make citron peel, which can be used in teas, infusions, and other herbal preparations, or being juiced to make a refreshing and potentially health-promoting drink.
